summary: Documentation reorganization and expansion for Story 16.3 (Example Caching & Performance). Four work packages: BR AC reorganization, implementation doc expansion (Clean Arch + Deployment + Performance), architecture.md caching patterns section, and cross-link validation.

key_changes: Reorganize AC into 10 clear, testable items (currently 5 scattered)
Add Business Rule: HMAC key rotation every 90 days with dual-key reads
Clarify which AC are behavioral (testable) vs. deployment (task)
Verify bidirectional cross-links to Epic 16 README and Implementation doc
questions_resolved: Q: Should HMAC rotation be AC? A: Yes, make it a Business Rule + AC item
Q: Is 'monitoring' too vague? A: Yes, be specific: 'Prometheus counter increments on hit'
Q: Does BR need Clean Arch discussion? A: No, defer to Implementation doc

new_sections.title: Clean Architecture: Wrapper Pattern Rationale
subsections: Why ExampleService should focus on business logic only
Why CachedExampleService wrapper handles cache orchestration

new_sections.title: Deployment Checklist
subsections: HMAC Key Rotation (Secret Manager + dual-key reads)
Load Testing (100 concurrent requests, hit rate capture)

new_sections.title: Performance & Cost Targets
subsections: Cache hit rate targets by date
Latency bounds (cache hit vs. cache miss)

new_sections.title: Technical Challenges & Solutions
subsections: Challenge 1: Cold cache hit rate on launch
Challenge 2: Duplicate Gemini calls under load
Challenge 3: HMAC key rotation during deployment

new_sections.title: Future Work & Standardization
code_examples: Usage example for decorated generator function

existing_sections_to_update.title: Technical Scope
existing_sections_to_update.change: Expand with file counts (6 new files, 3 test files, 2 configs)

existing_sections_to_update.title: Implementation Details
existing_sections_to_update.change: Verify 3 critical code examples are present and current

code_examples_required.name: HMAC Key Computation
code_examples_required.description: With secret rotation and version token

code_examples_required.name: Redis Lock Acquisition
code_examples_required.description: With unique lock token and TTL

files_to_update.estimated_content_change: 30% expansion — add 4 new sections + detailed deployment checklist

files_to_update.action: Add new section after Backend Architecture, before Frontend Architecture

new_section.title: Caching Patterns for Expensive Operations
subsections: When to Cache (decision criteria with ✅/❌ checklist)
cross_links: Links down to Story 16.3 BR/Impl docs
Referenced from new docs: Story 16.3, Stories 17-19
guidelines: Avoid story/epic numbers in high-level guidance
Use descriptive pattern names (Hybrid Lock + Cache vs. Story 16.3)
Provide decision framework for future APIs
Link to implementation details without prescribing

files_to_update.estimated_content_change: New section; no changes to existing content

validation_checklist: Epic BR links to Story 16.3 BR?
Story 16.3 BR links back to Epic BR?
Link text is consistent and descriptive?
Epic Impl links to Story 16.3 Impl?
Story 16.3 Impl links back to Epic Impl?
Link text is consistent and descriptive?

work_packages.name: Story 16.3 BR Reorganization
deliverables: AC items split into behavioral (testable) vs. deployment tasks
Business Rules section updated with HMAC rotation
acceptance_criteria: All AC items are testable/verifiable in code or tests
No vague statements (e.g., 'monitoring is set up')
Cross-links to Epic 16 README and Implementation doc

work_packages.name: Story 16.3 Implementation Expansion
deliverables: Clean Architecture section explaining wrapper pattern
Performance & Cost Targets table with concrete metrics
Technical Challenges & Solutions (4 challenges with code examples)
Future Work section on CacheableGenerationFactory + Epic 8 retrofit
acceptance_criteria: 3 critical code examples are present and syntactically correct
Performance metrics are concrete (%, ms, $, GB)
Technical Challenges include code patterns for each solution

work_packages.name: Architecture.md Caching Patterns Section
deliverables: New section: Caching Patterns for Expensive Operations (250–350 lines)
Decision criteria table (When to Cache vs. When Not to Cache)
Architecture diagram (textual or reference to visual)
3 design decision tables (hybrid vs. single, pattern components, tradeoffs)
acceptance_criteria: Guidance is generalizable to future APIs (Stories 17-19)
Cross-links to implementation docs are present
Decision framework helps developers answer 'should I cache this API?'

work_packages.name: Cross-Document Linking & Status Alignment
deliverables: Bidirectional cross-links verified (BR ↔ Impl, Epic ↔ Stories)
Status and Last Update fields synchronized
Architecture.md links to Story 16.3 and Epic 8 docs
Story 16.3 docs link back to Architecture.md caching section
acceptance_criteria: All links are relative markdown paths (no absolute URLs)
Status field is identical in BR and Implementation
Last Update date is identical in BR and Implementation
No broken links (test with markdown link checker if available)

key_design_decisions.decision: BR should NOT surface Clean Architecture wrapper pattern
key_design_decisions.rationale: BR is for stakeholders; implementation patterns belong in Implementation doc. BR states WHAT (caching + security + monitoring), not HOW.
key_design_decisions.impact: Clean Arch discussion is Implementation doc section, not BR. Architecture.md explains why it matters for portability.

key_design_decisions.decision: Exactly 3 core code examples in Implementation doc
key_design_decisions.rationale: Avoid overwhelming with examples. Three critical patterns: HMAC computation, Redis lock acquisition, GCS write with validation.
key_design_decisions.impact: Full service implementation shown separately; examples focus on patterns that differentiate this approach.

key_design_decisions.decision: Prometheus metrics are AC items; dashboard creation is deployment task
key_design_decisions.rationale: Metric emission is behavioral (testable in code); dashboard is operational (deployment concern).
key_design_decisions.impact: AC items focus on 'Prometheus counter increments' (verifiable). Deployment Checklist has 'Grafana dashboard created' (operational).

key_design_decisions.decision: Architecture.md has one comprehensive caching section, not decision trees
key_design_decisions.rationale: Provide framework for future APIs to decide 'should I cache?' without over-prescribing.
key_design_decisions.impact: Section includes decision criteria table (When to Cache) but not per-API decision trees. Keeps architecture doc lean.

key_design_decisions.decision: Architecture.md should link to specific code files (gcsCacheService.js, CachedConversationService.js)
key_design_decisions.rationale: Developers benefit from seeing where patterns live. Use relative paths and future tense to avoid prescriptiveness.
key_design_decisions.impact: Links point developers to implementation examples without mandating they be used for all cases.

curator_handoff_instructions: 1. Read full documentation design spec (story-16-3-docs-design.md) to understand all 4 work packages
2. Open the three files to update side-by-side: BR, Impl, Architecture
3. Work through packages sequentially (WP1 → WP2 → WP3 → WP4)
4. For each code example added, verify it can be copy-pasted and run
5. Test all markdown links (should resolve without broken URLs)
6. Validate Status and Last Update fields match across BR and Impl
7. Before commit: run final QA checklist (design spec section 'Success Criteria')
8. Merge all three file updates together (to maintain cross-document consistency)
quality_gates: ✅ All AC items in BR are testable/verifiable (no vague statements)
✅ Implementation doc includes all 5 required sections (Clean Arch, Deployment, Performance, Challenges, Future Work)
✅ All 3 code examples are syntactically valid (template can be copy-pasted)
✅ Architecture.md caching section avoids story/epic numbers (uses descriptive names)
✅ All cross-links are bidirectional and working
✅ Status and Last Update fields are identical in BR and Impl
✅ No non-template sections added (strict template compliance)
✅ Template structure is preserved (all required sections intact)
success_criteria: Documentation is complete when Docs Curator can implement all work packages without asking clarifying questions, all QA gates pass, and Appendix A (answers to key design questions) is understood.
